no cargo cap: enforce hold size or one trade breaks the economy
state.scene is 0-indexed; never write probes requiring scene>=1 at page load
blocking title screen makes harness see no input; start playable or make title key visibly change state
cards drawn as blank boxes: render rank+suit text on a rounded rect
hit-test bottom-up grabs a buried card: scan from the visually topmost down
launch velocity re-applied every frame: set once at launch, then only integrate
arc preview computed with different math than flight: reuse one step function
enemies steered by pixels not waypoints: track pathIdx + interpolation t, never freestyle
tower fires every frame: enforce a cooldown timer per tower
accepting swaps that produce no match: revert the swap
scanning only rows: check rows AND columns for runs
timing from frame counts: RAF jitters - always use elapsed milliseconds
the same note hittable twice: mark hit=true immediately
vision ignoring walls: do a segment-vs-rect intersection test for line of sight
instant detection feels unfair: use an alert meter that fills over ~0.5-1 s
unconnected rooms strand the player: connect sequentially so all are reachable
spawning entities inside walls: pick spawn cells from floor tiles only
ball tunnels through flippers at speed: substep the integration or cap speed
reflecting while still overlapping re-triggers: push the ball out of the shape first
costs not scaling: multiply by 1.07-1.15 per purchase or one generator dominates
per-frame income without dt: production becomes frame-rate dependent
hitbox = sprite size: use a 2-4 px core or it feels unfair
bullet array grows unbounded: cull off-screen aggressively and cap count
using e.code for letters: e.code is layout ('KeyA'); use e.key and filter length===1
case mismatch fails matches: normalize everything to lower case
requiring pixel-perfect landings: snap into place when offset is small - forgive
support computed against the whole stack: compare with the block directly below
importing three.js or WebGL for vector arcade: use 2D canvas stroke math only
emitting <assets> for vector games: everything is procedural lines
whole screen bright: the dark overlay was skipped — fill the canvas with dark rgba AFTER drawing the world
hard-edged circle of light: use createRadialGradient with the OUTER stop fully transparent for soft falloff
separate input paths for touch vs keyboard: feed ONE shared input object from both
touchstart-only handlers: use pointer events so mobile AND desktop both work
actionTimer decremented after an early-return: the action sticks forever — tick the timer BEFORE any return
action restarts every frame: start an action only on the keydown edge, not while the key is held
all blocks same color: hotbar must carry >=2 distinct type ids with different textures
break/place ray from player feet instead of camera crosshair

Run-and-gun side-scrollers (Metal Slug, Contra): the FIRE key spawns a projectile from the player's muzzle each press/interval, traveling in the player's current AIM direction — horizontal by facing sign, straight up when ArrowUp is held, diagonal when a direction+up are held. Firing is decoupled from movement: you can shoot while standing, running, jumping, or crouching. Keep all shots in ONE bullets[] array advanced in the main loop (b.x += b.vx; b.y += b.vy) and cull off-screen. Muzzle offset = player.x + facing*player.w, player.y + player.h*0.4. Do NOT gate firing behind on-ground or movement state; do NOT rewrite the movement/physics to add shooting — add a bullets array + fire handler only.

Gameplay-triggered outcome `<videos>` (KO/fatality, boss-defeat, goal-pole victory, game-over): when the trigger state is reached (hp<=0, reached goal, lives==0) PAUSE gameplay, show the muted skippable full-screen video overlay, and wire its `onended`/`onerror`/skip-key to a SINGLE continuation that returns to a known phase — usually the same `reset()`/`startGame()` path used by the R key (or advances to next round). Mirror of the intro rule: never leave the game stuck on the overlay if the video never fires. Reuse the ONE overlay loader used for the intro; don't build a second video system.

Two-fighter / versus games: give each fighter a stable prefix on every pose key (`f1`/`f2`, `p1`/`p2`, `blue`/`red`, etc.). BOTH fighters get the SAME pose suffixes in the same <assets> emit — truncating P2 to idle+special leaves pink MISSING boxes. In drawFighter, build the key as `prefix + '_' + phase` (never a bare action). On reset()/rematch, clear `_spriteCache` so rounds do not reuse stale Images.

SPATIAL ALIGNMENT (all graphic interaction games): every clickable/hittable region declares art-space source rect artSrc:{sx,sy,sw,sh} on the source PNG AND canvas draw rect via ONE shared mapper (gbox() or getBoundingClientRect scale). Hit-test and drawImage MUST call the same mapper — never mix e.offsetX/e.offsetY with unscaled canvas x/y. Until first green test, set window.__harnessAlignmentDebug=true (default) and draw debug overlays so VLM/harness can verify box↔art alignment.

Point-and-click hotspot alignment (NO measuring PNGs — text model cannot see art): generate EMPTY bg_* scenes (walls/floor/sky ONLY — no people, doors, items, NPCs baked in). Every clickable object is its own transparent sprite asset; each hotspot carries art:'<sprite_key>' and {x,y,w,h}; drawImage(sprite(h.art), h.x, h.y, h.w, h.h) every frame BEFORE hit-test so click region == visible object by construction. ONE shared mapper gbox(ax,ay,aw,ah) or getBoundingClientRect scale for BOTH drawImage AND pointer hit-test — never mix offsetX with unscaled canvas coords. drawHotspotDebug(sc) draws dashed rect + label on each hotspot when window.__harnessAlignmentDebug!==false. Expose window.__harnessHotspotCenters() returning [{id,cx,cy}] at rect centers. If a large fixed feature MUST stay in bg_* (e.g. tavern doorway), put coarse placement in the bg prompt ('door on right edge') AND set the hotspot rect to that same coarse region.

Point-and-click iter 1: expose `window.state = state` with `state.scenes` keyed by scene id — each `{ bg: '<empty_bg_key>', hotspots: [{id, art, x, y, w, h, on_click}] }`. Render order every frame: (1) drawImage active scene bg full-bleed, (2) for each hotspot drawImage(sprite(h.art), h.x, h.y, h.w, h.h), (3) player/NPC walk sprites on top. Hit-test the SAME {x,y,w,h} rects (topmost first). Do NOT bake interactive objects into bg_* — the sprite at the hotspot rect IS the clickable object. Opening-book `pointclick-puzzle-chain` hard-fails when `state.scenes` is missing — scaffold scenes + at least one hotspot in the first <html_file> even before puzzles work. Inventory: `state.inventory[]` + HUD strip; help overlay on H/?.

QTE hazard↔body alignment: each scene SCENES[i] has hazardPath:{from:{x,y},to:{x,y},threatens:'head'|'body'|'feet'|'sword'} plus cueMs/hitMs. Every frame drive threat sprite with hazardPosAt(sc,t01) where t01=(elapsed-cueMs)/(hitMs-cueMs) clamped 0..1 (ease with anticipation()). Hero pose must dodge the threatened part BEFORE impact. Expose window.__harnessThreatSample() returning {cue,nearHit,threatens,hero} normalized positions. drawDebugRoom() when __harnessAlignmentDebug.

Acceptance probe exprs must be SELF-CONTAINED: read only window.state / window.gameState / DOM / canvas, or call helpers the game explicitly attaches to window (window.simulateClick = …). Never call bare helpers like simulateClick(...) or distTo(...) — undefined helpers evaluate falsy every iter and burn the build (run_13 Elite Trader). Prefer inline KeyboardEvent / PointerEvent / real DOM button.click() inside the probe.

Snake acceptance probes that assert score/length growth MUST FORCE an eat: place food on the cell the head will enter next (state.food = {x:head.x+dx,y:head.y+dy}), dispatch the matching Arrow key, await 1–2 ticks, then assert state.score increased (or snake.length grew). Never idle-wait for a random eat — food may never land in front of the head within the probe window (run_14 Snake: score_increases_on_food waited 3s with score stuck at 1).

Dynamic probes must CAUSE the event they assert — set state, dispatch the key/click, then check the delta. A bare `await setTimeout` hoping score/food/collision happens is brittle and fails on correct games. Prefer: mutate the precondition (food cell, enemy position), fire input, await one tick, assert.

FPS/Doom kill→score probes must FORCE a kill, not bare-fire Space and hope: place one monster on the crosshair (same cell / in front of camera along look vector), set its hp to 1 (or call the game's damage/kill helper on window), then fire, await a tick, assert score/kills increased. A lone KeyboardEvent('Space') with no enemy in the ray fails on correct shooters (run_14 Doom: score_increases_on_kill saw score stuck at 0).

Pong/Breakout probes that assert ball motion MUST clear the serve gate first: set state.serving=false (or call serve()/launch()) before awaiting ball.x change. A ball parked at center with serving=true will never move — idle-wait probes fail on correct games (run_14 Pong: ball_moves saw ball.x=400 stuck).

Sprites that should read as white/light on a dark playfield must NOT be prompted as pure solid white — the asset pipeline chroma-keys near-white backgrounds and can erase the whole sprite, leaving ENTITY-NOT-RENDERED on a black canvas (run_14 Pong: white paddles/ball vanished). Prompt 'off-white / light gray paddle with a thin dark outline, transparent bg' instead of 'solid white'.

When the goal uses cutscene <videos> with image-to-video seeds: put key_intro / key_fail / key_victory (or the exact image names in <videos>) NEAR THE TOP of the <assets> list and keep the total sprite count within the per-turn cap (or use from_image chaining). FIFO overflow drops trailing names — if a video seed is dropped, cutscenes lose the locked character look (run_14 Dragon's Lair key_victory). Prefer fewer hazard extras over dropping keyart.

Harness auto-probes need window helpers on the FIRST <html_file>: QTE → window.__harnessThreatSample(); point-and-click → window.__harnessHotspotCenters() (and optional alignment debug). Missing helpers make injected probes fail every iter even when gameplay looks fine (run_14 QTE memory_gap). Attach helpers next to window.state.

Multi-floor sloped cascade hazards: track floor index; snap y to the surface; move toward the LOW end (canvas y down: yR>yL ⇒ vx>0). Set INITIAL vx from that slopeDir — never hardcode +vx. Leave gaps at ladder columns so hazards tumble mid-span; on tumble reverse vx. Full-width floors that only fall at x0/x1 do not cascade. Zigzag slopes; no free-fall skip.

Jump-over / hop-over hazard scoring (vertical arcade platformers, barrels/logs on girders): award points when the player is AIRBORNE (`!onGround`) AND horizontally overlaps the hazard AND the player's feet/bottom are ABOVE the hazard top — then mark that hazard `scored`/`passed` so it scores once. Check score BEFORE lethal collision so a successful hop does not also kill. Probe shape: push a hazard on the SAME platform surface (feet y, not player center y), dispatch Space, await ~800–1200ms through jump apex, assert `score` increased. Same-y center spawn + short wait often fails a correct scorer (run_15 Donkey Kong `jump_over_barrel_scores`).

STATIC-ACTION on ArrowLeft/ArrowRight (or WASD move) means the held locomotion pose is a single blit while the rest of the canvas still animates. Cycle >=2 distinct run/walk frames (or a continuous bob/leg swing) for the whole time the move key is held — do not hold `hero_run` / `walk` as one static frame. Attack STATIC-ACTION is covered separately; this is specifically for move keys (run_15 Donkey Kong ArrowLeft delta 0.0059).

Top-down RPG talk/NPC dialog: expose `state.dialogue.open===true` when Enter/talk fires while adjacent and facing an NPC. CRITICAL: `getFacedTile()` must use a facing KEY MAP matching player.facing strings — `const FACING={up:[0,-1],down:[0,1],left:[-1,0],right:[1,0]}` then `FACING[p.facing]`. A numeric array `[[0,-1],…]` indexed by `p.facing==='up'` is undefined and silently breaks talk/attack facing (run_15 Zelda: dialog_opens falsy while npcs exist; probe sets tx/ty + facing='up' + Enter). Also wire keydown→pressed.talk and do not let attack/act return before the talk check.

Versus fighter punch damage probes place fighters in range, fire punch, assert defender hp drops. Implement: during attack-active frames only, test attacker hitbox vs defender hurtbox; on hit set per-attack `hasHit` so one punch cannot multi-hit; reduce hp once. Wire the SAME punch key the probe dispatches (often KeyA/KeyZ/Space — match Phase A). Do not require a long animation before damage if the probe waits ~200–400ms (run_15 Street Fighter punch_deals_damage stayed falsy at 6/8).

Grid-maze chase: cycle chomp/mouth frames while the round is playing — do not gate on dir!=0 (looks frozen). Chasers must keep a non-zero dir and advance every frame. Single-frame ghosts need eye offset or a tiny bob. Prefer drawImage when sprites loaded.
